Hey, welcome to module number one, Cracking the Code for Sales and Influence. Now this is where I break down the straight line into its most basic elements and also show you the tools of human influence that were used to actually take someone down the straight line as the phrase goes, from the open to the close. Now remember, like I said in the introduction, you have that very strong, good enough factor with the straight line. So once you become even reasonably proficient at it, you'll find yourself with a massive increase in your close rate and also you'll be setting yourself up to create a massive number of customer referrals. So with that, let's dive right in by going back to that diagram I drew of the actual straight line after I said those five very profound words about how every sale is the same. So again, I drew that long, thin line across the center of the whiteboard and I placed a big, thick X on either end. And I said, now this is your open, which is where the sale begins. And this is your close, where the prospect says, yeah, let's do it. And he opens up the account with you. And the key here is that from literally the first word out of your mouth, everything you say and everything you do is designed to keep your prospect on the straight line and slowly nudging him or her forward towards the close. Now as a salesperson, every once in a while we get one of those perfect lay down sales where the prospect seems to be almost pre sold before we even open up our mouths. I'm talking about one of those sales where basically everything you say and everything you do, the prospect keeps saying yes, yes, yes, without giving you even a single objection right up to the moment where he says, yeah, after you asked him for the order. That's what I mean by a perfect straight line sale. Now the problem is that those sales are far and few between. You see what typically happens is that your prospect keeps trying to take you off the straight line and take control of the conversation, which is basically the death to the sales process. So you want to keep the prospect on the straight line, moving forward towards the close, but he or she keeps trying to take you off the straight line and spying off the Pluto up here or down here to your anus, which is not a very good place to be. At least not for most of us. So what we have here are these healthy boundaries, both above and below the line. You have one here and one here. Boom, boom, these two spots. That's where the magic actually happens when you're actually off the straight line, but inside these boundaries in these two spaces. So the key is to stay inside these healthy boundaries where you remain in control of the sale. Now when you're actually on the straight line, meaning you're directly on it, that's where you're doing all the talking. And you have all these little arrows here, here, here, right on the straight line. And the reason they're all pointing down the line towards the close is because with every single word you say, you have one specific goal line, which is to move the prospect down the straight line towards the close. That's it. There are no free words, no time for stupid statements, and no time to go off the Pluto and talk about the price of tea in China. That's what novices do. When you're speaking, it's directed, it's powerful, your words have meaning behind them. And the meaning is to create massive certainty in the mind of your prospect as you move him or her down the straight line from the open to the close. That's why every one of these arrows is compact and it's tight and it's right on the line. They're all pointing towards the close. So again, that's what's happening when you're directly on the line. You're doing all the talking and your prospect is doing the listening. And when you're off the straight line but still inside these boundaries here and here, your prospect is doing the talking and you're doing the listening. And by the way, this is where some really great stuff is happening. When you're actually off the straight line but right in these two spaces. In fact, there's not one, but two absolutely crucial things happening here. First, you're developing immediate and massive rapport on both a conscious and unconscious level and second, you're gathering massive intelligence. Now in the past, you might refer to that as qualifying but with the straight line system, we refer to it as gathering intelligence. You see, in point of fact, when you're gathering intelligence from a prospect with a straight line system, you're identifying all the following things. Number one, you're identifying their needs, not just their core need but also any secondary or tertiary needs or problems they might have. Number two, you're identifying any core beliefs they might have that could impact the sales such as not feeling comfortable working over the phone or making a quick decision or also not trusting salespeople in general. Number three, you want to find out about any past experiences they've had with similar products both good and bad and also how they feel about the individual salesperson that they bought from in that case. Number four, you want to identify their values, meaning what things are most important to them in terms of their overall outcome. Number five, you want to identify their financial situation insofar as what level of wealth or spending ability they have as it relates to your product. And number six, you want to identify where their pain lies, meaning what's keeping them up at night? Just that one single worry that sits at the base of their skull like an anchor and weighs them down. You see, at the end of the day, knowing where someone's pain lies and if necessary, amplifying that pain if they're currently in denial is going to be instrumental in helping you close the tougher sales. So going back to the straight line now, when you're actually off the straight line, you're looking to A, continue building on the rapport that you've already created and B, you want to use that rapport to help you gather even more massive and more invasive intelligence. Essentially, those are the three basic tenants of the front half of the straight line system. Number one, you must take immediate control of the sale. That is absolutely a must. Number two, you must engage in massive intelligence gathering. And number three, as you're gathering that intelligence, you're going to do it in a way that allows you to build even more rapport with your prospects on both a conscious and an unconscious level. So again, during the front half of the sale, you're first going to take immediate control of the sale and don't worry, I'll show you exactly how to do that. And then second, you're going to use that control to gather massive intelligence which entails asking your prospects specific questions, which I'll hand you on a silver platter, as I said before. And then third, you're going to use that intelligence gathering phase to get the even tire rapport with your prospects on both a conscious and an unconscious level, which then sets you up for massive success during the rest of the sale. And there you have it, my friend. Those are the three basic tenants of the front half of the straight line system. And now it's quiz time. So you know what you've got to do. And after you're done, I'll see you in the next lesson.
